search:go-gin-swagger:gin framework swag.content:
- **CLI Commands**:
  - `go get -u github.com/swaggo/gin-swagger`
  - `go get -u github.com/swaggo/files`

- **Latest Versions**:
  - Gin Framework: v1.8.2 (as of my last update)
  - Swagger UI for Go: v3.0.0

- **Key Config Options**:
  - Define routes and their corresponding handlers.
  - Use annotations to document your API endpoints.
  - Configure middleware for authentication, logging, etc.
  - Set up the Swagger route to serve the UI.

Example of setting up Swagger with Gin:

```go
package main

import (
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    swaggerFiles "github.com/swaggo/files"
    ginSwagger "github.com/swaggo/gin-swagger"
)

// @title Sample API
// @version 1.0
// @description This is a sample API.
func main() {
    r := gin.Default()

    // Define your routes here

    r.GET("/swagger/*any", ginSwagger.WrapHandler(swaggerFiles.Handler))

    r.Run(":8080")
}
```

This setup includes:
- Basic Gin router initialization.
- Swagger documentation annotations.
- Route for serving the Swagger UI.

- **CLI Commands**:
  - `go get -u github.com/swaggo/gin-swagger`
  - `go get -u github.com/swaggo/files`

- **Latest Versions**:
  - Gin Framework: v1.8.2
  - Swagger UI: v4.1.3

- **Key Config Options**:
  - `@title`: API Title
  - `@version`: API Version
  - `@description`: Description of the API
  - `@host`: Host for the API documentation
  - `@schemes`: Supported schemes (e.g., http, https)
  - `@basePath`: Base path for the API

- **Example Code Snippet**:
  ```go
  package main

  import (
      "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
      swaggerFiles "github.com/swaggo/files"
      ginSwagger "github.com/swaggo/gin-swagger"
  )

  // @title Sample API
  // @version 1.0
  // @description This is a sample API server.
  func main() {
      r := gin.Default()

      r.GET("/swagger/*any", ginSwagger.WrapHandler(swaggerFiles.Handler))

      r.Run(":8080")
  }
  ```

- **Additional Notes**:
  - Ensure your Go code is annotated with Swagger tags for documentation.
  - Run `swag init` to generate the `docs.go` file containing the Swagger documentation.

search:go-gin-swagger:generating api doc.content:
- Install `swaggo/gin-swagger` using `go get -u github.com/swaggo/gin-swagger`
- Use `swag init` to generate Swagger documentation
- Add `@swagger` annotations in your Go code for API documentation
- Import `github.com/swaggo/gin-swagger/swaggerFiles` and use it as middleware in Gin router
- Ensure `ginSwagger.DefaultOptions()` is configured with the correct URL path
- Update `main.go` to include Swagger UI integration
- Run your application using `go run main.go`
- Access Swagger UI at `/swagger/index.html`

- **CLI Commands**:
  - `swag init`: Generates Swagger documentation.
  - `go get -u github.com/swaggo/gin-swagger`: Updates gin-swagger package.

- **Latest Versions**:
  - Gin: v1.8.0 (as of my last update)
  - Go-Swagger: v1.24.0 (as of my last update)

- **Key Config Options**:
  - `@api` tags in your Go code to document API endpoints.
  - `swagger.json` and `swagger.yaml` files generated by `swag init`.
  - Middleware setup in Gin to serve Swagger UI (`gin-swagger.WrapHandler`).

search:go-gin-swagger:optimizing rest ap.content:
- **CLI Commands**:
  - `go get -u github.com/swaggo/gin-swagger`
  - `go get -u github.com/swaggo/files`

- **Latest Versions**:
  - Gin: v1.8.2
  - Swagger: v1.6.0

- **Key Config Options**:
  - Use `// @title` for API title.
  - Use `// @version` for API version.
  - Use `// @description` for API description.
  - Use `// @host` to specify the host.
  - Use `// @BasePath` to set the base path.

- **GitHub Repository**: [swaggo/gin-swagger](https://github.com/swaggo/gin-swagger)
- **Latest Version**: Check the repository for the latest release tag.
- **Key Config Options**:
  - `ginSwagger.WrapHandler(swaggerFiles.Handler)`: Wraps the Swagger handler with Gin middleware.
  - `@swagger:route [GET] /users users getUsers "Get all users"`: Adds a comment to document an API endpoint.
  - `@Param id path int true "User ID"`: Adds a parameter description for Swagger documentation.
- **CLI Commands**:
  - `go get -u github.com/swaggo/gin-swagger` (to install the Gin-Swagger middleware)
  - `go get -u github.com/swaggo/files` (to install the Swagger UI files)

These steps and configurations will help you integrate Swagger into your Gin-based REST APIs for better documentation.
